title: Ex-Yankee Sonny Gray earns first victory against New York as member of Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray earns his first win against the Yankees as a Red Sox, pitching 6.1 innings in a 5-3 victory.
Gray, who played for the Yankees in 2017-18, expressed his dislike for New York upon joining Boston.
In 11 starts this season, Gray holds a 7-1 record with a 3.20 ERA and 1.24 WHIP for the Red Sox.

title: Willson Contreras rides highs and lows of Red Sox win over Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a 2-run homer and had a heated moment in the dugout during Red Sox's win over Yankees.
Contreras contributed early with an RBI single, ending the game 2 for 4 with 3 RBIs in total.
Interim manager Chad Tracy praised Contreras for his leadership and performance, noting his influence on younger players.

title: Severe thunderstorms could hit Mass. Saturday evening, forecasters say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Severe thunderstorms with hail and damaging winds are expected in Massachusetts Saturday afternoon and evening.
Storms are predicted to develop after 2 p.m. due to a cold front moving through Southern New England.
Greatest risk for severe weather is in areas west and northwest of Interstate 95, including possible localized flooding.

title: Nantasket Beach stayed quiet despite fears of a social media-fueled ‘takeover’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hull police increased patrols at Nantasket Beach following social media rumors but reported no incidents on Friday.
Authorities noted a quieter than usual Nantasket Beach, despite concerns over a rumored large gathering.
Similar social media-fueled events elsewhere in New England have led to disturbances; no issues reported at Carson Beach.

title: Red Sox’ Payton Tolle talks ‘playing with joy’ for self, team: ‘I try to come with a smile every day’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Payton Tolle, Red Sox pitcher, debuted on August 29, 2025, against the Pittsburgh Pirates, showcasing strong emotions and a high-velocity fastball.
Tolle uses his excitement strategically to build team momentum, often celebrating big plays on the mound and drawing on his love for the game.
On Mother's Day, Tolle honored his late mother by playing with joy, a mantra she encouraged, during a challenging game against the Tampa Bay Rays.

title: Colin Cowherd: Celtics future feels ‘weird’ compared to sustainable Knicks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Colin Cowherd criticizes the Celtics for being overly dependent on Tatum and Brown's contracts, which limit their financial flexibility.
The Knicks are praised for balanced team structure and flexibility, with Jalen Brunson's team-friendly contract cited as a key advantage.
Cowherd contrasts the Celtics' struggles in the fourth quarter with the Knicks' strong finish, led by Brunson's consistent performance.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Hoppers' on Disney+ stars Piper Curda as Mabel, a teen using technology to connect with animals and save nature.
'Pillion' on HBO Max features Harry Melling and Alexander Skarsgård in a non-traditional romcom about a BDSM relationship.
'The Vampire Lestat' on AMC+ continues Anne Rice's story, focusing on Lestat's flamboyant rise as a rockstar vampire.

title: Red Sox move Garrett Crochet to 60-day IL as ace continues to deal with discomfort
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox place Garrett Crochet on 60-day injured list due to lat tightness, extending his absence until at least June 25.
Crochet's move opens a 40-man roster spot, allowing Red Sox to add reliever Joe La Sorsa ahead of the Yankees series.
Brayan Bello is demoted to Triple-A Worcester amidst struggles, leaving Boston's rotation reliant on other starters.

title: Endicott baseball falls to Denison in hard-fought DIII  National Championship Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Endicott College's baseball team lost 4-3 to Denison in Division III National Championship Game 3, finishing as runner-up.
Endicott forced Game 3 after a 11-10 walk-off win in extra innings, overcoming a 6-2 deficit against Denison.
The championship marked Endicott baseball's first appearance in a national title game, after four consecutive College World Series.

title: Laxative-laced brownies rattle Nantucket School Committee meeting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A Nantucket resident presented laxative-laced brownies at a School Committee meeting to protest artificial turf proposal.
Meghan Perry linked the brownies to PFAS concerns, comparing them to trace laxatives in the dessert.
School officials filed a police report about the incident; no brownies were consumed, and charges are unclear.
